title: kb note published
description: Emitted deliberately (via `kb event emit` or a jsonl append) when an agent session pushes a knowledge-vault note or design asset to the phone surface. NOT fired on every vault write — the semantic is 'this doc is for you, go read it'. `kb_ref` is the vault-relative slug without the .md extension (e.g. market-ops/2026-07-17-some-note), matching hearth-api `GET /api/kb/indexed/{*slug}`; hermes turns it into a `hearth://kb/{kb_ref}` notification deep link after re-validating it. `design_ref` is a `{project}/{file}` pointer (extension kept, .html or .md) into hearth-api `GET /kb/design/{project}/{slug}` — a self-contained live example/mockup; hermes maps it to `hearth://design/{design_ref}`. Exactly one of the two should be set; hermes prefers kb_ref if both are. Everything else in the payload stays on the card, never on the notify rail.
